title: Dockerized Build Environment for Parallel Agent Development
description: Create a Docker-based development environment that allows multiple AI agents to develop mesh nodes in parallel, each in their own isolated container. Each container must be able to: (1) compile the Java codebase, (2) run the mesh viewer scene with headless OpenGL rendering (Mesa llvmpipe or Xvfb), (3) expose the automation API on a unique port, (4) execute CLI validation commands against its own instance. This enables N agents working on N different node tickets simultaneously without port collisions, shared state, or build interference. Modeled after standard CI/CD container patterns but optimized for iterative agent development rather than one-shot builds.
definition-of-done: 1. Dockerfile at docker/Dockerfile builds the full Ixdar codebase with Java 21, Maven, LWJGL. 2. Container can run the mesh-viewer scene headlessly with software rendering (Mesa llvmpipe or Xvfb + LWJGL). 3. docker-compose.yml defines a template service that can be scaled to N instances. 4. Each container gets a unique automation API port (e.g., 47832 + container_index) via environment variable. 5. The automation CLI can target a specific container via --base-url. 6. A helper script (docker/agent-env.sh) starts a container, runs compilation, launches the scene, and outputs the base URL. 7. Containers can be torn down cleanly. 8. README.md in docker/ documents usage for agents.
testing-plan: 1. Build the Docker image: 'docker build -t ixdar-dev docker/'. 2. Start two containers on different ports. 3. Run 'mvn -DskipTests compile' inside each, verify success. 4. Launch mesh-viewer in each with different automation ports. 5. Run 'ixdar_cli.py --base-url http://localhost:PORT health' against each, verify both respond. 6. Run 'ixdar_cli.py --base-url http://localhost:PORT screenshot' against each, verify both produce screenshots. 7. Tear down containers, verify clean shutdown.
todos: Create docker/ directory at repo root
Write Dockerfile: Java 21 base, Maven, LWJGL native libs, Mesa for software rendering
Configure Xvfb or Mesa llvmpipe for headless OpenGL in container
Write docker-compose.yml with scalable service definition and port mapping
Parameterize automation API port via -Dixdar.automation.port environment variable
Write docker/agent-env.sh helper script: build, launch scene, output base URL
Write docker/teardown.sh for clean container shutdown
Write docker/README.md with agent usage instructions
Test that LWJGL loads and MeshShader compiles inside the container
Test that screenshots work with software rendering
unknowns: Whether LWJGL works with Mesa llvmpipe out of the box or requires specific LWJGL configuration flags
Whether to use Xvfb (virtual X server) or EGL offscreen rendering for headless GL
Whether to prebuild the Maven dependencies in the Docker image or mount the local .m2 cache
Whether agents need git inside the container or should mount the workspace read-write
